items.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach

children.content: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

articles.title: Seasons clash as polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map

children.content: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

image.altText: A graphic of Australia with a cyclone icon on WA's west coast and a blue mass above Tasmania and far south-east.

articles.title: Track Cyclone Narelle live: Where is it forecast to cross WA coast?

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it heads towards the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

gallery.altText: The latest satellite image of Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

Two people filling sandbags with red dirt

items.title: Review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws

children.content: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

image.altText: A collage of three photos of people with their faces scratched out.

items.title: Fremantle among defendants in expanding AFL concussion class action

children.content: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

image.altText: Fremantle Dockers fans

items.title: The WA musician set to become world's oldest band conductor

children.content: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

image.altText: A man in a blue shirt sits conducting an orchestra

items.title: Ships are escaping the Strait of Hormuz and one is headed for WA

children.content: The Japanese-owned Iron Maiden will be the first known ship to dock in Australia after it was stranded in the Persian Gulf when the US and Israel first bombed Iran on February 28.

gallery.altText: A large ship floating in a dock.

thumbnail.altText: A large ship floating in a dock.

items.title: Hard time for renters as landlords squeeze tight market

children.content: Australian renters continue to have a hard time finding a home, despite vacancy rates slowly improving from post COVID pandemic lows, a report finds.

image.altText: A For Lease sign advertising a rental property in Carlton North.

items.title: Police charge public servant who allegedly helped partner skip housing waitlist

children.content: A 55-year-old public servant is accused of illegally offering a social housing tenancy to her intimate partner, allowing them to skip a large number of applicants who were in the queue for a home.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair carrying a designer bag and mobile phone walks outside court

items.title: Debt-laden council agrees to sell mining camp for $22m

children.content: It was supposed to be a cash cow amid yet another mining boom, but the Shire of Coolgardie is selling its controversial Bluebush Village for $22.05 million, as part of plans to clear massive debts.

image.altText: The entrance to a mining camp with sign showing name of village.

items.title: Adelaide looking to end 23-year drought in blockbuster Geelong clash

children.content: Adelaide heads to Geelong as a rank outsider looking to spring an upset for the first time at Kardinia Park since 2003. Follow live.

image.altText: Izak Rankine and Oisin Mullin battle for a loose ball

items.title: Drone baiting aims to eradicate mice infestation on remote WA island

children.content: Scientists are using "world-first" drone-baiting technology to target thousands of house mice in an effort to bring migratory seabirds back from the brink on Browse Island off Western Australia's north.

gallery.altText: close up of green bait pellets coming out of a drone in the sky
A dead mouse and a hole

thumbnail.altText: close up of green bait pellets coming out of a drone in the sky
A dead mouse and a hole
flock of birds over researchers on white sandy beach

items.title: 'My left foot was in his mouth': Fisher's lucky escape from 3.5m shark

children.content: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

image.altText: A man a black and red t-shirt holds two rock lobsters in front of a boat

items.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk, North Adelaide Football Club, about to kick a ball in an Aussie rules match

items.title: We've been here before as global crises tend to follow a predictable path

children.content: If the mass uncertainty sparked by the military escalation in Iran is making you feel anxious, you are not alone — but there is some calming news for us all.

image.altText: A man holds a petrol nozzle next to his car.

items.title: What should you eat to keep your brain sharp as you get older?

children.content: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

image.altText: A bowl of salad - cherry tomatoes, lettuce, sweet potato, capsicum and cabbage

items.title: Here's who you should see at comedy festivals across Australia

children.content: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

gallery.altText: A TV still of Aaron Chen, 30, Chinese Australian, in glasses, performing on stage, smiling and clasping a mic in both hands.

thumbnail.altText: A TV still of Aaron Chen, 30, Chinese Australian, in glasses, performing on stage, smiling and clasping a mic in both hands.

avatar.alt: Hannah Story, a blonde woman sitting outside smiles. A dog is at her feet, and she wears a T-shirt that reads "MIRANDA JULY".

items.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz, parteth the seceund: Test thine knowledge once more

children.content: Is this a quiz which you see before you?  We're back with another 10 questions to test your knowledge of William Shakespeare's plays.

image.altText: A film still of Claire Danes, 17, in angel wings, holding the hand of Leonardo DiCaprio, 22, dressed as a knight.

items.title: AFL says new illicit drug policy is 'most stringent in Australian sport'

children.content: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

image.altText: Darcy Moore and Andrew Dillon at a press conference

items.title: Rain wrecks day one of Shield final after South Australia's top order buckles

children.content: Victoria sent South Australia in to bat on day one of the Sheffield Shield final and claimed three wickets before rain stopped play.

image.altText: Alex Carey prepares to bat for South Australia in the Sheffield Shield final

items.title: 'Here I am': Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female Archbishop of Canterbury

children.content: Dame Sarah Mullally is enthroned as the Church of England's first female Archbishop of Canterbury, taking the helm as the spiritual leader of 85 million Anglicans worldwide in a historic ceremony blending tradition with global symbolism.

image.altText: A woman wearing gold robes and gold ceremonial headgear sits on an ancient stone chair.

items.title: Basil Zempilas not ruling out preference deal with One Nation at WA election

children.content: Basil Zempilas is "not fearful" of the soaring rise of Pauline Hanson's One Nation in the west, but acknowledges his WA Liberals will need to decide whether to direct preference votes to the party or distance themselves.

gallery.altText: Basil Zempilas smiles while sitting down
Basil Zempilas speaks to a reporter while sitting down
A long-range shot of Basil Zempilas speaking to Courtney Withers in a studio sit down interview

items.title: PM calls second emergency national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis

children.content: States and territory leaders will convene next week to further coordinate the national response to the fuel crisis as the fallout from the Iran war continues to escalate.

image.altText: Albanese wearing suit and tie looks serious

items.title: 'Fight will continue' without change, say ABC workers amid 24-hour strike

children.content: Unions representing ABC staff have signalled the possibility of further strike action if a dispute over pay and conditions at the broadcaster isn't resolved.

image.altText: People strike and hold up signs.

items.title: Alleged fuel thief driver struck worker who tried to stop him, court hears

children.content: An alleged fuel thief accused of seriously injuring a Perth petrol station worker in a hit-and-run is told he will likely go to prison if convicted of the "extremely serious" charges.

image.altText: A free petrol bowser at a Vibe service station on a sunny day.

items.title: In the final stage of Ryan's young life, his family says he was let down

children.content: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

image.altText: A man and woman holding a photo of their son.

items.title: 'So lucky': Burke warns failed Perth bomb attack could have killed many

children.content: The home affairs minister says Australia was "so lucky" to avoid mass casualties during the attempted Australia Day bomb attack in Perth and admits the nation was slow to recognise the gravity of the alleged terrorist act.

gallery.altText: A crowd of people gather in central Perth to protest against Invasion Day.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of people gather in central Perth to protest against Invasion Day.
